file_name: VAPOTHERM, INC. - Manufacturing and Supply Agreement.txt

perturbation.original_text: 3.3 Delivery. Each shipment of Cartridges will be delivered by [* * *] to the applicable Vapotherm manufacturing facility or retained in Medica's warehouse facility, in accordance with Vapotherm instructions for each shipment. Medica shall deliver by the delivery date specified in a purchase order all of the Cartridges specified in that purchase order. Vapotherm is only required to pay for Cartridges actually delivered. Medica shall make shipping arrangements with carriers designated in writing by Vapotherm from the [* * *] point to points specified by Vapotherm, under the arrangements that Vapotherm has with those carriers.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.3 Delivery. Each shipment of Cartridges will be delivered by [* * *] to the applicable Vapotherm manufacturing facility or retained in Medica's warehouse facility, in accordance with Vapotherm instructions for each shipment. Medica shall ensure prompt delivery by the estimated delivery date acknowledged on the purchase order for all of the Cartridges specified in that purchase order. Vapotherm is only required to pay for Cartridges deemed shipped. Medica shall make shipping arrangements with carriers designated in writing by Vapotherm from the [* * *] point to points specified by Vapotherm, under the arrangements that Vapotherm has with those carriers.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states Medica 'shall deliver by the delivery date'. The modified text changes the obligation to 'ensure prompt delivery by the estimated delivery date acknowledged on the purchase order'. The terms 'estimated delivery date' and 'delivery date' are used interchangeably. Furthermore, the original text indicates 'Vapotherm is only required to pay for Cartridges actually delivered' whereas the modified text indicates 'Vapotherm is only required to pay for Cartridges deemed shipped'. The in-text contradiction is between guaranteeing a specific delivery date versus an estimated date, also requiring payment on shipped carts versus actual delivered carts.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.3 Delivery. Each shipment of Cartridges will be delivered by [* * *] to the applicable Vapotherm manufacturing facility or retained in Medica's warehouse facility, in accordance with Vapotherm instructions for each shipment. Medica shall ensure prompt delivery by the estimated delivery date acknowledged on the purchase order for all of the Cartridges specified in that purchase order. Vapotherm is only required to pay for Cartridges deemed shipped. Medica shall make shipping arrangements with carriers designated in writing by Vapotherm from the [* * *] point to points specified by Vapotherm, under the arrangements that Vapotherm has with those carriers.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a contradiction regarding delivery and payment obligations. The original text requires 'delivery by the delivery date' and payment for 'Cartridges actually delivered.' The changed text weakens the delivery obligation to 'ensure prompt delivery by the estimated delivery date' and changes the payment trigger to 'Cartridges deemed shipped.' This directly conflicts with the original requirement of actual delivery for payment, creating an ambiguity about when payment is due and what constitutes acceptable delivery. The terms 'estimated delivery date' and 'delivery date' are misaligned, and payment upon 'deemed shipped' versus 'actually delivered' creates further inconsistency and a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 4.1 Conformity with Specifications. Any Cartridges that Medica manufactures under this Agreement must conform to the specifications in Exhibit D (the "Specifications") and (2) be manufactured, labeled, packaged, stored, and tested (while in the possession of, stored by, or under the control of Medica) in accordance with cGMP. Medica shall provide adequate packaging for protection during normal shipping and handling environments.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1 Conformity with Specifications. Any Products that Medica manufactures under this Agreement must adhere to the guidelines in Exhibit D (the "Specifications") and (2) be manufactured, labeled, packaged, stored, and tested (while in the possession of, stored by, or under the control of Medica) in accordance with cGMP. Medica shall provide adequate packaging for protection during normal shipping and handling environments.
perturbation.explanation: The original text uses 'Cartridges' and the changed text uses 'Products'. It is unclear if 'Cartridges' and 'Products' are the same thing. The text goes on to reffering to 'Cartridges' in the same section. The confusion arises because the contract is for the manufacturing of 'Cartridges' and not 'Products'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.3 Rejection. Vapotherm may reject any Cartridge that does not meet the Specifications or otherwise comply with this Agreement (any such Cartridge, a "Nonconforming Cartridge"). If Medica accepts that Vapotherm was entitled to reject the Nonconforming Cartridge(s) and Vapotherm has already paid the purchase price for the Nonconforming Cartridge(s), then, within [* * *] Calendar Days after receiving notice from Vapotherm under Section 4.2, Medica shall, at Vapotherm's election, either replace the Nonconforming Cartridge(s) at no additional cost to Vapotherm or reimburse Vapotherm for the purchase price of the Nonconforming Cartridge(s) via wire transfer. Further, if Medica accepts that Vapotherm was entitled to reject the Nonconforming Cartridge(s) and Vapotherm has not already paid the purchase price for the Nonconforming Cartridge(s), then, within [* * *] Calendar Days after receiving notice from Vapotherm under Section 4.2, Medical shall, at Vapotherm's election, either replace the Nonconforming Cartridge(s) at no additional to Vapotherm beyond the original purchase price charged to Vapotherm for the Nonconforming Cartridge(s) or cancel that purchase order or the portion thereof relating to the Nonconforming Cartridge(s), s applicable. (b) If Medica does not agree that one or more Cartridges constitute Nonconforming Cartridges, the Joint Review Committee, consisting of quality assurance representatives from both companies, must consider the matter. If after consideration by the Joint Review Committee the parties are unable to reach agreement within [* * *] Calendar Days after the date Medica received notice from Vapotherm under Section 4.2, they shall submit the dispute to arbitration in accordance with Section 14.5.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Cartridges' to 'Products' in Section 4.1 creates a contradiction with Section 4.3, which specifically refers to 'Cartridges' and 'Nonconforming Cartridges'. The change introduces ambiguity and uncertainty as to whether the rejection and replacement terms outlined in 4.3 apply to 'Products' as defined in the changed Section 4.1. This ambiguity directly conflicts with the specificity of Section 4.3, making it unclear whether the original terms still apply, thus leading to a strong in-text contradiction and falling under the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 9.4 Definition of Recall. For purposes of this Article 8, "recall" means any action by Vapotherm or any of its Affiliates, or either Medica or any of its Affiliates, to recover title or possession or halt distribution or use of any Cartridges sold or shipped to any other Persons. The term "recall" also applies to Cartridge that would have been subject to recall if it had been sold or shipped.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.4 Definition of Recall. For purposes of this Article 9, "recall" means any action by Vapotherm or any of its Affiliates, or either Medica or any of its Affiliates, to recover title or possession or halt distribution or use of any Cartridges sold or shipped to any other Persons. The term "recall" also applies to Cartridge that would have been subject to recall if it had been sold or shipped.
perturbation.explanation: The original text references 'Article 8', when it is referring to the article in-text 'Article 9', so this creates an in-text contradiction. Making the reader unsure to where it is reffering to.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.4 Definition of Recall. For purposes of this Article 9, "recall" means any action by Vapotherm or any of its Affiliates, or either Medica or any of its Affiliates, to recover title or possession or halt distribution or use of any Cartridges sold or shipped to any other Persons. The term "recall" also applies to Cartridge that would have been subject to recall if it had been sold or shipped.
perturbation.justification: The original text references 'Article 8' in the definition of 'Recall,' but the changed text corrects it to 'Article 9.' The contradicted text also references 'Article 9.' Therefore, the original text creates a contradiction with the contradicted text, as it incorrectly identifies the article number relevant to the definition of recall. This creates confusion, making the reader unsure which article it refers to.
